title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Greenpoint, Brooklyn
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Greenpoint, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Greenpoint residents facing bat infestations need fast, professional solutions to protect their homes and families. Our specialized bat removal and exclusion services safely eliminate bat colonies while preventing future re-entry into your property. With extensive experience serving Brooklyn's northernmost neighborhood, we understand the unique challenges presented by Greenpoint's mix of historic rowhouses, converted warehouses, and modern developments.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Greenpoint for over a decade, providing humane, effective bat removal that complies with all New York State wildlife regulations. Our certified technicians use proven exclusion methods tailored to Greenpoint's diverse architectural styles, ensuring long-term protection for your property.
localContext: Greenpoint's proximity to Newtown Creek and the East River creates ideal bat habitats, while the neighborhood's century-old buildings and converted industrial lofts offer numerous entry points for roosting colonies. From McGolrick Park to the waterfront, our team knows every corner of Greenpoint and responds quickly to bat emergencies throughout the 11222 zip code.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Greenpoint?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Greenpoint typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before any work begins.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Brooklyn?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bats are protected wildlife in New York State. Professional removal must be done humanely using exclusion methods that allow bats to leave but not return. We follow all state and local regulations, and timing restrictions apply during maternity season (May-August) when flightless pups are present.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Greenpoint home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include scratching or squeaking sounds at dusk or dawn, dark staining around potential entry points, small droppings (guano) in attics or walls, and spotting bats entering or exiting your building at twilight. You may also notice a musty ammonia-like odor from accumulated guano.

faqs.question: How long does the bat exclusion process take?
faqs.answer: Most bat exclusion projects in Greenpoint take 2-4 weeks to complete. We install one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit safely, monitor for 7-14 days to ensure all bats have left, then seal all entry points permanently. Timeline varies based on colony size and building accessibility.
